headline: MA63 Review Stalls After 7 Years of Talks
context: The MA63 steering committee formed in 2019 completed only 4 of 21 agenda items by March 2026, frustrating Sabah and Sarawak leaders.

cards.big: Only 4 of 21 MA63 agenda items resolved since 2019
cards.sub: Seven years of negotiations produced minimal results for Borneo states

cards.big: Sarawak Premier Abang Johari demanded full review completion by 2027
cards.sub: GPS threatened to reassess federal coalition support if timeline slips

cards.big: Immigration autonomy restored in 2022 but customs powers still pending
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak want full control over 17 remaining jurisdictional areas

cards.big: Constitutional amendment in 2021 restored equal partner status on paper
cards.sub: Critics say the amendment lacks enforcement mechanisms and judicial clarity

cards.big: The MA63 delay reveals whether federal power-sharing is genuine or performative
Only 4 of 21 MA63 items resolved — the process itself reveals who holds real power.
